data.narrative:
Planet Labs is a public-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Chris Boshuizen, William Marshall, and Robbie Schingler. The valuation analysis relies on a peer-median framework across three same-sector, same-stage public comparables: Rocket Lab ($37.0B), Firefly Aerospace ($6.3B), and Astroscale ($900M).

The peer-median method is the primary applicable approach, yielding an implied valuation of $6.3B based on the median of the three comparables. The peer universe spans a wide range—$900M at the low end to $37.0B at the high end—reflecting material dispersion in public Space & Aerospace valuations. A public-comp anchoring approach using the sector's 64.2x median price-to-sales multiple across six public tickers could theoretically apply but cannot be executed without Planet Labs' disclosed revenue, a data gap typical of private-stage companies.

The triangulated valuation range is $5.4B to $7.2B, with a median of $6.3B. This range sits slightly below the company's stated $7B+ valuation. Key uncertainties include the wide peer dispersion—the high comp at $37.0B trades at a 41x premium to the low—and the absence of revenue-based valuation anchors. The narrow methodology count (one applicable method) limits confidence; access to additional financial metrics or a broader peer set would strengthen the analysis.
